user_query:
Our monthly revenue reconciliation job failed early this morning, and I need it back to a reliable state for our finance tracking. Please investigate and resolve the incident in this local workspace.

What I need you to do:
1) Read logs/reconcile-2026-04-15.log and extract the UTC timestamp of the failure and the error signature from the last error in the file.
2) Reproduce the failure locally by running: python scripts/reconcile.py --config config/finance.json. Capture the full stderr from that failing run to artifacts/repro_stderr.txt so we have a record of the behavior.
3) Inspect scripts/reconcile.py and the config file to determine the cause of the failure. Do not modify scripts/reconcile.py. Apply the minimal configuration change needed to make the job run successfully.
4) After the fix, re-run the job with the same command and verify it completes and produces out/revenue_summary.csv. Capture stdout and stderr from the successful run to artifacts/success_run.txt.
5) Create a concise incident report at artifacts/incident_report.json with these fields: {"incident":"monthly_revenue_reconcile","failed_at_utc":"<from log>","error_signature":"<from log>","root_cause":"<brief plain-English explanation>","fix_applied":"<exact change to config/finance.json>","verification":{"output_file":"out/revenue_summary.csv","totals":{ "USD":"<from revenue_summary.csv>", "EUR":"<from revenue_summary.csv>" }}}.

Constraints and notes:
- Only change configuration; do not change scripts/reconcile.py or input data files.
- All artifacts must be saved exactly at: artifacts/repro_stderr.txt, artifacts/success_run.txt, artifacts/incident_report.json, and the job’s output at out/revenue_summary.csv.
- Use only local files and standard shell/python tools. No internet is required.
- The task is complete when the incident report is created, the failing behavior is reproduced and captured, the configuration is corrected, and out/revenue_summary.csv is present with currency totals. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
